scored_hypotheses.claim: The manuscript uses a null-derived word-construction rule (e.g., a gallows-glyph prefix system) such

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: The 70.1% hapax ratio strongly contradicts the hypothesis; a productive prefix-stem system should produce repeated combinations, not mostly one-time-only words. High glyph entropy (3.86 bits) is also inconsistent with 5 glyphs dominating >60% of tokens. The shallow Zipf exponent alone is insufficient to support the hypothesis without contradicting evidence.
